---
title: "Self Care"
format: html
---

# Self-Care as Relapse Prevention

Self-care is not indulgence; it is risk management. Sleep loss, poor nutrition, inactivity, and chronic stress increase emotional reactivity and cravings. When baseline health drops, relapse risk rises.

A strong self-care plan includes basic body care, emotional care, and practical care. The most useful plans are simple enough to repeat even on hard days.

![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/sleep.jpeg?raw=true){fig-align="center"}

## Self-Care Baseline Checklist

-   [ ] I get enough sleep most nights (7–9 hours).
-   [ ] I go to bed and wake up at consistent times.
-   [ ] I eat regular meals (not skipping breakfast, lunch, or dinner).
-   [ ] I include balanced nutrients (protein, carbs, fats, fruits/vegetables).
-   [ ] I hydrate throughout the day (water or non-caffeinated drinks).
-   [ ] I limit excessive caffeine or sugary drinks.
-   [ ] I move my body most days (walking, stretching, or exercise).
-   [ ] I spend some time outside or get natural light daily.
-   [ ] I take breaks to rest when I feel physically tired.
-   [ ] I pay attention to hunger, fullness, and body signals.
-   [ ] I maintain basic hygiene (showering, brushing teeth, clean clothes).
-   [ ] I take medications or supplements as prescribed.
-   [ ] I limit exposure to environments that increase physical stress (e.g., loud, crowded, or high-risk settings).
-   [ ] I reduce screen time before bed to support sleep quality.
-   [ ] I practice at least one physical calming skill (deep breathing, stretching, grounding).
-   [ ] I allow my body time to recover after stressful events.
-   [ ] I ask for support before I reach physical or emotional exhaustion.

::: callout-caution
### Try This

Choose a box you left unchecked and turn them into this today's goal.
:::
